meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
family.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 4. Occupations classified in TEER 4, as those included in this group with a 4 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of secondary school; or several weeks of on-the-job training with some secondary school education; or experience in a related occupation from TEER 5, when applicable (TEER 5 includes occupations with a 5 as a second digit in their code).
family.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
family.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

units.title: Mail and parcel sorters and related occupations
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Mail and parcel sorters and workers in related occupations process and sort mail and parcels in post offices, mail processing plants, warehouses and internal mail rooms. They are employed by Canada Post Corporation, courier and parcel express companies and establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Mail and parcel sorters and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Mail sorters process and sort mail and parcels in post offices, mail processing plants and internal mail rooms.
main_duties: Sort mail according to destination
Bundle, label, bag and route sorted mail to the proper delivery stream
Operate vehicles and material handling equipment in order to sort mail and load postal trucks
Search directories to find the correct address for redirected mail.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school and some college office administration courses may be required.
On-the-job training is required.
Physical fitness may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or facility management positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Mail and parcel sorters and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Mailroom clerks receive, process, sort and distribute manually or electronically incoming and outgoing mail, faxes, messages and courier packages; maintain address databases and produce personalized mailings.
main_duties: Receive, process, sort and distribute incoming and outgoing mail, faxes, messages and courier packages manually or electronically
Maintain address databases and produce personalized mailings
Print and photocopy material, stuff envelopes and affix address labels
Weigh outgoing mail, calculate and affix proper postage, and bundle by postal code
May operate and maintain print shop and mailing house equipment.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school and some college office administration courses may be required.
On-the-job training is required.
Physical fitness may be required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory or facility management positions is possible with additional training or experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Letter carriers sort and deliver mail, record delivery of registered mail and collect money for cash-on-delivery parcels. They are employed by Canada Post Corporation.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Letter carriers sort and deliver mail, record delivery of registered mail and collect money for cash-on-delivery parcels.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is required.
Two weeks to one month of training is provided by Canada Post Corporation.
Physical fitness is required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training or experience.

core_competencies.statement: to understand instructions clearly and accurately, asking questions when necessary, which helps in meeting customer expectations for mail delivery
to recognize and respond appropriately to the needs and concerns of customers during deliveries, fostering effective communication and positive interactions
to perform tasks such as walking while sorting and handling mail, managing package deliveries and operating postal equipment
to lift heavy mailbags, walk long distances and perform repetitive bending and twisting motions
to accurately sort mail by address, follow specific delivery instructions, record registered mail deliveries and leave notices for undeliverable items
to understand route navigation, mail sorting, mail delivery timing and safety compliance

units.title: Couriers and messengers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Couriers and messengers pick up and deliver letters, parcels, packages, messages and other items within and between establishments. They are employed by courier service companies and other establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Couriers and messengers pick up and deliver letters, parcels, packages, messages, and other items within and between establishments.
employment_requirements: Secondary school education may be required.
Eligibility for bonding may be required.
Ability to navigate streets and follow directions is usually required.
Physical fitness is required for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: There is some mobility into other delivery occupations such as delivery drivers.
There may be mobility to clerical occupations within the company.
Progression to supervisory positions may be possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to plan routes efficiently, ensuring mail is delivered on time and in the correct sequence
to clearly communicate addresses, delivery instructions and time estimates to customers or dispatch teams
to quickly interpret and prioritize verbal instructions, written messages, electronic signals and physical cues to efficiently navigate dynamic environments
to lift and carry heavy packages efficiently, maintain balance while navigating diverse terrains and manage physically demanding delivery schedules
to consistently provide accurate delivery of parcels, verify recipient information and meticulously follow delivery instructions
to understand routes, time management, package handling and simple tracking and documentation for efficient transport and delivery of packages

units.title: Railway yard and track maintenance workers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workers
units.minor_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Railway yard workers regulate yard traffic, couple and uncouple trains and perform related yard activities. Railway track maintenance workers operate machines and equipment to lay, maintain and repair railway tracks. They are employed by railway transport companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Railway yard and track maintenance workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Railway yard workers regulate yard traffic, couple and uncouple trains and perform related yard activities
main_duties: Operate control panel switches from railway yard tower to set traffic signals and to activate train switches that regulate railway traffic in railway yards
Operate car retarders, turntables and track switches to align locomotives and cars as required for coupling, uncoupling or servicing
Switch cars according to instructions which indicate the location, disposition and number of cars
Lubricate moving parts of railway cars and locomotives.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
Experience as a railway labourer is required.
Railway yard workers require a Canadian Rail Operating Rules certificate.
additional_information: Progression to a position such as brakeman is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Railway yard and track maintenance workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Railway track maintenance workers operate machines and equipment to lay, maintain and repair railway tracks.
main_duties: Operate machines and equipment such as sleeper sliders, anchors, rail lifters, drag drills, drag diggers, rail saws and tie-bar cranes to lay, maintain, repair and align railway tracks to transfer, spread, level and tamp ballast around ties and rails and to clear snow from tracks
Clean and make minor repairs to machines and equipment
Patrol assigned track sections to identify and report damaged or broken track
Perform other track maintenance duties as directed.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided
Railway yard workers require a Canadian Rail Operating Rules certificate.
additional_information: Progression to a position such as brakeman is possible with experience.

units.title: Water transport deck and engine room crew
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workers
units.minor_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Water transport deck and engine room crew stand watch, operate and maintain deck equipment, perform other deck and bridge duties and assist ship engineer officers to operate, maintain and repair engines, machinery and auxiliary equipment aboard ships or self-propelled vessels. They are employed by marine transportation companies and federal government departments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Water transport deck and engine room crew
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Engine room crew - water transport assist ship engineer officers to operate, maintain and repair engines, machinery and auxiliary equipment aboard ships and self-propelled vessels.
main_duties: Assist ship engineer officers in performing routine maintenance work and repair to ship's engine, machinery and auxiliary equipment
Monitor engine, machinery and equipment indicators, record variables and report abnormalities to ship engineer officer on watch
Lubricate moving parts of engines, machinery and auxiliary equipment
Operate and maintain off-loading liquid pumps and valves
Maintain engine parts and keep engine room clean.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: Training and testing for various endorsements and certification, including the Marine Emergency Duty (MED) certificate, occur following employment as a deck crew member.
Senior positions in this unit group, such as able seaman/woman, require experience.
Progression to deck or ship engineer officer positions is possible with experience, additional training and deck officer certification by Transport Canada.

example_titles.job_title_text: engine and boiler room crew member - ships

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Water transport deck and engine room crew
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Deck crew, water transport stand watch, operate and maintain deck equipment and perform other deck and bridge duties aboard ships and self-propelled vessels under the direction of deck officers.
main_duties: Stand watch and steer ship or self-propelled vessel under the direction of the officer on watch
Operate, maintain and repair deck equipment such as winches, cranes, derricks and hawsers
Clean, chip and paint deck surfaces
Handle mooring lines, and splice and repair ropes, wire cables and other cordage
Load, discharge and secure ship cargo.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: Training and testing for various endorsements and certification, including the Marine Emergency Duty (MED) certificate, occur following employment as a deck crew member.
Senior positions in this unit group, such as able seaman/woman, require experience.
Progression to deck or ship engineer officer positions is possible with experience, additional training and deck officer certification by Transport Canada.

units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workers
units.minor_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Air transport ramp attendants operate ramp-servicing vehicles and equipment, handle cargo and baggage and perform other ground support duties at airports. They are employed by airline and air services companies and the federal government.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Air transport ramp attendants operate ramp-servicing vehicles and equipment, handle cargo and baggage and perform other ground support duties at airports.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience operating baggage transporting equipment or warehouse experience may be required.
A driver's licence and a good driving record are usually required.
additional_information: There is mobility among jobs in this unit group.

core_competencies.statement: to operate ramp-servicing vehicles and equipment such as towing tractors, food service trucks, de-icer sprayers and lavatory servicing trucks
to track the movement of baggage and cargo, monitor equipment operations and identify potential hazards
to load and unload cargo and baggage and to transport freight among aircraft and airport warehouses
to lift and move cargo, operate ground support equipment and navigate safely in a dynamic airport environment
to coordinate with colleagues in facilitating smooth loading, unloading and servicing of aircraft, maintaining schedules and adhering to safety protocols

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Vehicle, Machinery and Equipment Operations
core_competencies.statement: to understand basic ground service equipment, such as tugs, belt loaders, ramps and pushback tractors focusing on safe handling and routine operational tasks

units.title: Automotive and heavy truck and equipment parts installers and servicers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workers
units.minor_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Automotive and heavy truck and equipment parts installers and servicers install replacement automotive mechanical parts such as mufflers, exhaust pipes, shock absorbers, springs and radiators and perform routine maintenance service such as oil changes, lubrication and tire repairs on automobiles, trucks and heavy equipment. They are employed by automobile and truck service and repair shops, service departments of industrial establishments and construction, mining and logging companies.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Automotive and heavy truck and equipment parts installers and servicers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Automotive and heavy truck and equipment parts installers and servicers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Automotive and heavy truck and equipment parts installers and servicers install replacement automotive mechanical parts such as mufflers, exhaust pipes, shock absorbers, springs and radiators and perform routine maintenance service such as oil changes, lubrication and tire repairs on automobiles, trucks and heavy equipment.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school is usually required.
Several weeks of on-the-job training are usually required.
additional_information: There is some mobility among installers and servicers in this unit group.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Automobile and truck service and repair shops

example_titles.job_title_text: lubrication technician - automotive service and repair
crane and dragline oiler and greaser

core_competencies.statement: to diagnose, repair and replace vehicle components and perform preventative maintenance tasks such as inspecting, lubricating and adjusting parts to ensure proper functioning and prevent breakdowns

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to monitor equipment performance, such as checking engine diagnostics, detecting and troubleshooting irregularities like hydraulic leaks
to accurately identify and pinpoint the sources of sounds and vibrations within vehicles, facilitating effective diagnosis and repair mechanical or electrical problems
to use core strength to lift, maneuver and install heavy components and equipment, also requiring twisting and bending to ensure safety and precision during maintenance and repair tasks
to accurately identify and address mechanical issues, follow precise specifications for part installations and repairs
to understand part identification, simple mechanical systems, routine tasks and safety procedures

units.title: Utility maintenance workers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workers
units.minor_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Utility maintenance workers locate, repair, install and perform maintenance work to various equipment and facilities, such as water filtration and distribution plants, waste water treatment plants and power plants. They may also work on exterior and underground gas mains and distribution lines. They are employed by municipal, provincial and federal public works departments, private contractors under contract with government public works departments and gas distribution companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Waterworks maintenance workers locate, repair, install and perform maintenance on waterworks equipment and facilities.
main_duties: Check, clean and lubricate waterworks equipment, such as pumping equipment, chlorination equipment and compressors
Adjust and repair waterworks equipment under the direction of qualified tradesperson
Search and locate reported water leaks and repair water-mains, valves or outlets
Install water meters using hand and power tools.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Several years of experience as a labourer in the same company may be required.
On-the-job training is provided.
Drinking water certification is required in Quebec and Ontario for waterworks maintenance workers.
additional_information: Mobility between jobs in this unit group normally occurs within the same area of work, such as within waste water treatment plants, within water filtration and distribution plants or within gas distribution plants.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Gas maintenance workers  locate, repair, install and perform maintenance and minor repairs to exterior and underground gas mains and distribution lines.
main_duties: Check and lubricate gas pipeline valves
Conduct routine survey of gas mains and distribution lines to detect and locate escaping gas using gas detecting devices
Investigate reports of gas leaks to determine the exact location and extent of leaks
Dig ground to expose gas lines and repair damaged pipes
Keep record of work performed and location and condition of pipelines
Provide information on underground gas distribution lines and observe excavation work to ensure that underground gas facilities are protected.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Several years of experience as a labourer in the same company may be required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: Mobility between jobs in this unit group normally occurs within the same area of work, such as within waste water treatment plants, within water filtration and distribution plants or within gas distribution plants.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

units.title: Public works maintenance equipment operators and related workers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workers
units.sub_major_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workers
units.minor_group_title: Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related maintenance workers
units.definition: Public works maintenance equipment operators and related workers operate vehicles and equipment to maintain streets, highways and sewer systems and operate trucks to collect garbage and recyclable materials. This unit group also includes workers who clear vegetation close to power lines. They are employed by municipal, provincial and federal public works departments, private contractors under contract with government public works departments and private companies involved in the collection of refuse and recyclable materials.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Public works maintenance equipment operators and related workers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Public works maintenance equipment operators and related workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Public works maintenance equipment operators and related workers operate vehicles and equipment to maintain streets, highways and sewer systems and operate trucks to collect garbage and recyclable materials. This unit group also includes workers who clear vegetation close to power lines.
employment_requirements: Experience as a public works labourer is usually required.
A driver's licence appropriate to a specific type of equipment may be required.
On-the-job training is provided.
Trade certification for utility arborists is available, but voluntary, in Ontario and British Columbia.
Some secondary school may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Municipal, provincial and territorial, and federal public works departments
Private contractors under contract with government public works departments

example_titles.job_title_text: urban snow removal equipment operator - public works
snow removal equipment operator - public works
sewer jet machine operator - public works
sewer jet cleaner operator - public works

core_competencies.statement: to drive garbage trucks, street cleaning equipment such as street sweepers and other vehicles equipped with rotating brushes, snowploughs and plough blades

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to ensure any potential issues or malfunctions in machinery and equipment are detected early, preventing accidents that could harm the operator, other workers, or the public
to quickly and precisely adjust garbage truck, snowplows or other equipment controls to maintain speed and positioning
to manipulate levers and other controls to remove garbage, refuse and snow from streets, highways, parking lots and similar areas
to accurately follow procedures, operate and maintain equipment safely and maintain public infrastructure standards

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Vehicle, Machinery and Equipment Operations
core_competencies.statement: to understand safe, efficient and accurate operation and maintenance of specialized machinery like snowplows, graders and dump trucks
